description_long: This is a tall, narrow barn with a steeply pitched gable roof and a prominent central chimney. The front facade features a mix of half-timbered framing and plaster infill, with visible wooden beams creating a grid pattern. There are several windows of varying sizes, some grouped together and others spaced apart, and a single main door at the base. The side walls are mostly plain plaster, with minimal fenestration and a large expanse of uninterrupted surface. The roof is covered in textured shingles, and the overall structure has an old-world, European rural character. The barn's asymmetrical footprint and distinctive roofline give it a unique silhouette among urban or village buildings.
description: A tall, narrow barn with a steep gable roof, half-timbered front, and a central chimney. Features include a mix of windows, a single main door, and textured shingle roofing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the half-timbered facade, main entrance, several windows, and the steep gable roof with a central chimney.
description_view_1: Side view reveals a mostly plain plaster wall with minimal windows and the full length of the shingled roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a simpler facade with fewer windows, the continuation of the gable roof, and a small extension at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the large, uninterrupted plaster wall and the roof's overhang, with minimal architectural detailing.
attribute_siding_pattern: half-timbered with plaster infill on the front, plain plaster on the sides and rear
attribute_door_style: single hinged door with a rectangular design at the front base
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple windows of varying sizes, some grouped and some spaced apart, primarily on the front and rear facades
